source.title: The effect of spatially inhomogeneous extracellular electric fields on neurons

confidence_rationale: Direct biophysical modelling of the very regime MECH-270 needs: endogenous fields associated with hippocampal theta and sharp waves, their effect on pyramidal-cell spike timing. This is a stronger mapping to MECH-270 than Anastassiou 2011 (cortical slice) because the field sources modelled are hippocampal theta and SWR fields specifically, not generic extracellular fields. The conclusion -- that local fields generated by the cooperative action of brain cells can influence the timing of neural activity at physiological amplitudes -- is the existence proof MECH-270 needs, delivered in hippocampus. Confidence is discounted because this is a modelling paper (using morphologically realistic neurons rather than recorded cells in vivo) and because the modelled field effects on spike timing do not directly demonstrate a 'stream-local coherence readout' function.
failure_signatures: If simulated endogenous theta- and SWR-amplitude fields did not measurably affect spike timing of pyramidal cells, MECH-270's proposal that field coherence is the physical substrate for per-stream verisimilitude would have no biophysical basis in the hippocampus.

mapping.source_claim_statement: Endogenous electric fields associated with hippocampal theta and sharp waves can greatly affect spike timing of pyramidal cells, as shown by biophysical simulation of morphologically realistic neurons in spatially inhomogeneous extracellular fields. Local fields generated by the cooperative action of brain cells influence the timing of neural activity.
mapping.ree_translation: MECH-270 proposes ephaptic field coherence in CA1/CA3 pyramidal layers as the physical readout for per-stream verisimilitude. This paper establishes the biophysical link in hippocampus specifically: at the field amplitudes actually present during theta and SWR, spike timing is measurably affected. The 'coherent-population-produces-coherent-field' logic MECH-270 depends on requires that fields at realistic hippocampal amplitudes can modulate firing; Anastassiou et al. 2010 is the targeted biophysical demonstration that they can.
mapping.mapping_caveat: This is modelling on morphologically realistic neurons, not direct in vivo recording of ephaptic coherence during SWRs. The paper establishes that field effects at hippocampal-physiological amplitudes affect spike timing; it does not demonstrate that stream-local ensemble coherence encodes prediction-realization alignment. That functional interpretation -- the core MECH-270 claim -- is an extrapolation from the biophysical existence proof.
mapping.source_context: Biophysical simulation using one-dimensional cable models and morphologically realistic pyramidal neuron reconstructions; field sources include measured hippocampal theta and sharp-wave extracellular potentials; simulations conducted in collaboration with Buzsaki lab data.

confidence_components.notes: J Neurosci; senior authors include Buzsaki and Koch; rigorous biophysical modelling grounded in measured hippocampal field recordings. Mapping fidelity higher than Anastassiou 2011 because the modelled field source is hippocampal theta/SWR specifically, not generic cortical fields. Transfer risk low because the target regime (hippocampus) matches the claim directly; main discount is that this is simulation-plus-measurement rather than direct population-coherence recording.
